title: Client Self-Service Portfolio Summary Dashboard
problem_statement: Clients currently have no single view of their total portfolio performance across all account types (brokerage, IRA, 529). They must navigate between three separate screens and manually reconcile figures, leading to a 22% call center spike on market-volatility days when clients cannot self-serve basic performance questions. Financial advisors spend an average of 8 minutes per call restating information the client could read themselves if it were surfaced clearly.

goals.goal: Reduce avoidable call center volume on market-volatility days
goals.success_measure: Call center volume attributable to portfolio-balance questions down ≥ 20% within 60 days of full rollout
goals.measurement_method: Compare call reason codes month-over-month via Genesys call-tagging report

goals.success_measure: Dashboard page views per active client ≥ 3 per month; session duration on dashboard ≥ 90 seconds median
goals.measurement_method: Adobe Analytics dashboard-page event tracking; 30-day rolling median

goals.goal: Surface consolidated portfolio value without requiring manual reconciliation
goals.success_measure: 100% of clients with ≥ 2 account types see a single aggregated total on the dashboard without navigating away
goals.measurement_method: Feature flag rollout verification + automated smoke test on account-aggregation endpoint

personas.name: Retail Client
personas.role: Individual investor with one or more Client-ABC accounts (brokerage, IRA, or 529)
personas.goal: Understand total portfolio value and day-over-day performance in under 30 seconds without calling their advisor
personas.pain_point: Forced to visit three separate screens and add up balances manually; no single number tells them how they are doing today

personas.name: Financial Advisor
personas.role: Client-ABC advisor reviewing a client's account ahead of a scheduled review call
personas.goal: See the same summary view the client sees so conversations are grounded in shared numbers
personas.pain_point: Clients reference figures from the old separate screens; advisors cannot quickly find which screen the client is looking at

personas.name: Branch Office Administrator
personas.role: BOA who handles inbound calls when the advisor is unavailable
personas.goal: Answer basic portfolio-balance questions by navigating to the same view the client has
personas.pain_point: Currently must pull three separate screens while the client waits; no consolidated view exists in the internal tool

functional_requirements.description: The dashboard must display a single aggregated total portfolio value summing all account types the authenticated client owns (brokerage, IRA, Roth IRA, 529, annuity)
The dashboard must display day-over-day dollar change and percentage change for the aggregated total, updated as of market close each trading day
Each individual account must be listed below the aggregate with its own balance, day change, and account type label
The dashboard must display a 12-month performance sparkline for the aggregated portfolio value
Clients must be able to select a date range (1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, YTD) to filter the sparkline and the performance delta figures
The Financial Advisor view of the dashboard must display the same aggregated figures as the client view when the advisor navigates to the client's account in the advisor portal
Data must indicate its as-of timestamp (e.g. 'As of market close Jun 30, 2026') on every figure that is not real-time

performance: Dashboard initial load (all accounts aggregated) must complete within 2 seconds at p95 on a 4G mobile connection
Account aggregation API response must be ≤ 500ms p95 under a load of 50,000 concurrent authenticated sessions
Sparkline data must render within 1 second of date-range selection change (client-side, data already fetched)
accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across all dashboard components
All charts must have a text-based alternative (data table toggle) for screen reader users
Color-alone must not be the sole indicator of positive/negative performance — use icons + color
security: All portfolio data served over TLS 1.3 minimum
Account balances must not appear in browser history, cache, or URL parameters
Advisor portal view requires explicit client-account-access permission scope in the OAuth token — no ambient access
No PII or account numbers in client-side analytics events (Adobe Analytics)
scalability: Account aggregation service must handle horizontal scale-out to support peak load on high-volatility market days (estimated 3x normal traffic)
Sparkline data endpoint must support pagination for clients with > 20 accounts
availability: Dashboard must be available 99.95% of the time during market hours (6 AM – 8 PM ET, Mon–Fri)
Degraded mode: if account aggregation service is unavailable, show individual account balances from cache with a staleness warning rather than a blank dashboard
compliance: Performance figures must include required FINRA disclosure language per compliance-disclosure-requirement-D-14
As-of timestamp required on all non-real-time figures (FR-7)
Audit log entry required for every advisor access to client dashboard view
observability: Emit dashboard_loaded, account_aggregation_failed, and date_range_selected events to Datadog RUM
P95 latency SLO alert in PagerDuty if account aggregation p95 exceeds 800ms for 5 consecutive minutes

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Authenticated client sees aggregated total on dashboard load
acceptance_criteria.given: an authenticated client with accounts of types brokerage, IRA, and 529 navigates to the portfolio dashboard
then: a single aggregated total portfolio value is displayed prominently at the top of the page
the value equals the sum of all account balances as of the most recent market close
the value is formatted as USD currency with two decimal places

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Day-over-day change is displayed with correct sign and color
acceptance_criteria.given: an authenticated client is on the portfolio dashboard
acceptance_criteria.when: the dashboard renders the aggregated performance section
then: a dollar change figure is displayed showing the difference from the previous trading day's close
a percentage change figure is displayed alongside the dollar change
positive changes are indicated with an upward arrow icon and green text
negative changes are indicated with a downward arrow icon and red text
and: an icon (not color alone) distinguishes positive from negative to meet WCAG AA non-text contrast

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Individual account breakdown is visible below aggregate
acceptance_criteria.given: an authenticated client with multiple accounts is on the portfolio dashboard
acceptance_criteria.when: the client scrolls below the aggregated total section
then: each account is listed with its account type label (e.g. 'Traditional IRA', 'Brokerage')
each account shows its individual balance as of most recent market close
each account shows its individual day-over-day dollar and percentage change

acceptance_criteria.scenario: As-of timestamp appears on all non-real-time figures
acceptance_criteria.given: an authenticated client is on the portfolio dashboard
acceptance_criteria.when: the dashboard renders any balance or performance figure
then: a timestamp label reading 'As of market close [Month DD, YYYY]' appears adjacent to each figure
the timestamp reflects the actual last market close date, not the current date

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Advisor sees the same aggregated view as the client
acceptance_criteria.given: a Financial Advisor with client-account-access permission navigates to a client's portfolio dashboard in the advisor portal
then: the aggregated total displayed matches the value the client would see for the same client account
an 'Advisor View' banner is displayed to distinguish the advisor's session from a client session

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Advisor without permission cannot access client dashboard
acceptance_criteria.given: a Financial Advisor whose OAuth token does not include client-account-access scope for a specific client
acceptance_criteria.when: the advisor attempts to navigate to that client's portfolio dashboard
then: the advisor is shown an 'Access not authorized' message and prompted to request access through the appropriate channel

in_scope: Aggregated portfolio total across all Client-ABC account types (brokerage, IRA, Roth IRA, 529, annuity)
Day-over-day dollar and percentage change for aggregated total
Per-account balance breakdown with individual day change
As-of timestamp on all non-real-time figures (compliance requirement D-14)
Financial Advisor portal view showing same aggregated data as client view
WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for all new dashboard components
Degraded-mode graceful fallback when aggregation service is unavailable

out_of_scope.rationale: Requires a separate real-time data feed contract and infrastructure investment; tracked in CABC-5102

out_of_scope.item: Transaction history and trade confirmation display
out_of_scope.rationale: Separate concern owned by the Transactions team; this epic covers summary/performance only

out_of_scope.item: Tax-lot level detail and unrealized gain/loss breakdown
out_of_scope.rationale: Deferred to CABC-5210 (Tax Insights epic); adds significant complexity to the aggregation model
Mobile app is a separate release track; this epic covers the responsive web experience only
Multi-client aggregation is a different data model; separate epic CABC-5044

tech_stack.styling: Client-ABC Design System (internal Tailwind-based component library v3.2)
tech_stack.state: TanStack Query v5 for server state; Zustand for UI state
tech_stack.charting: Recharts 2 (already approved in design system — do not introduce D3 directly)
tech_stack.auth: OAuth 2.0 + PKCE via Client-ABC Identity Platform (Okta-backed)
tech_stack.api: REST over HTTPS; account aggregation endpoint at /api/v3/portfolio/summary

existing_patterns: Account card component already exists in the design system (ClientABCDesignSystem/AccountCard) — extend, do not fork
All data fetches use the useSuspenseQuery pattern from TanStack Query — do not use useEffect for data loading
Currency formatting must use the shared formatCurrency() utility from @client-abc/utils — do not roll a custom formatter
Feature flags managed via LaunchDarkly; flag name convention is client_abc_feature_<slug>
integration_points: GET /api/v3/portfolio/summary?clientId={id} — returns aggregated balance + per-account breakdown; owned by Accounts Platform team
GET /api/v3/portfolio/history?clientId={id}&range={range} — returns time-series data for sparkline; same owner
Adobe Analytics: fire client_abc_dashboard_loaded and client_abc_date_range_selected events on interaction
Datadog RUM: dashboard_loaded and account_aggregation_failed custom actions
Client-ABC Identity Platform: OAuth token must include portfolio:read scope for client data and portfolio:advisor_read for advisor access
deployment_constraints: Blue/green deployment via Client-ABC Kubernetes platform; all changes must be feature-flagged under client_abc_feature_client-portfolio-summary-dashboard for zero-downtime rollout
Lighthouse CI p95 LCP ≤ 2.5s gate runs on every PR — regressions block merge
No direct database access from the frontend BFF — all data via the Accounts Platform REST API
SOC 2 audit logging for advisor access events must be written to the existing audit-log Kafka topic before the API response is returned

figma.label: Portfolio Dashboard — Final UX Designs (all breakpoints)
figma.notes: Handoff-ready as of 2026-06-20; includes desktop, tablet, and mobile-web breakpoints. Component annotations link to Client-ABC Design System tokens.

figma.label: Degraded Mode & Error State Designs
figma.notes: Covers aggregation-service-unavailable, partial-data, and session-timeout states

architecture_diagrams.notes: Review before designing the API client layer; note the existing caching strategy at the BFF layer

architecture_diagrams.label: Client-ABC Client Portal — Target State System Context Diagram
architecture_diagrams.notes: Shows identity platform, Adobe Analytics, and Datadog RUM integration points

github_repos.notes: Existing account list page — the new dashboard replaces this. Study the existing data-fetch patterns before introducing new ones.
AccountCard component to extend for the per-account breakdown rows
OpenAPI spec for the /api/v3/portfolio/summary and /history endpoints — read this before building the API client

spec_documents.label: Compliance Disclosure Requirement D-14 — Performance Figures
spec_documents.notes: Mandates as-of timestamp and specific FINRA disclosure language on all performance figures

spec_documents.label: Client-ABC Accessibility Standards — WCAG AA Implementation Guide
spec_documents.notes: Internal implementation guide with Client-ABC-specific patterns for chart accessibility

examples.label: Reference: Settings Page Redesign (approved TanStack Query + Suspense pattern)
examples.notes: Use this PR as the reference implementation for data fetching patterns — reviewed and approved by architecture

examples.label: Reference: LaunchDarkly feature flag integration pattern
examples.notes: Shows the approved pattern for wrapping features in LD flags with a fallback — copy this, do not invent a new pattern

existing_apis.label: Portfolio Summary API — OpenAPI Spec
existing_apis.notes: Live API catalog entry; includes sandbox environment URL and auth token scopes required

existing_apis.label: Portfolio History API — OpenAPI Spec
existing_apis.notes: Supports range param: 1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, YTD. Max data points per response: 365.

risks.description: Account aggregation API latency may exceed 2s p95 SLO on high-volatility market days when all clients refresh simultaneously
risks.mitigation: Implement client-side stale-while-revalidate caching via TanStack Query (5-minute TTL); BFF response caching layer already exists — confirm cache TTL with Accounts Platform team before sprint 1
risks.trigger: Accounts Platform p95 latency alert fires in Datadog for ≥ 3 consecutive minutes during market hours
risks.contingency: Activate degraded mode: serve last-cached values with staleness warning; suppress sparkline data fetch to reduce API load

risks.description: Compliance disclosure language (D-14) interpretation may require legal review and delay the as-of timestamp implementation
risks.mitigation: Engage compliance team in sprint 1 design review; get written sign-off on the proposed as-of timestamp format before implementation begins
risks.trigger: Compliance team raises an objection to the proposed disclosure format after implementation is underway
risks.contingency: Feature-flag the performance delta figures independently from the balance display; can ship balance view while disclosure language is finalized

risks.description: Client-ABC Design System AccountCard component may not support all required display variants (annuity account type label, degraded-mode state) without modifications
risks.mitigation: File a design system component request in sprint 1; schedule a pairing session with the design system team to scope the extension
risks.trigger: Design system team confirms the required variants cannot be added within the epic timeline
risks.contingency: Fork AccountCard as a local component within client-portal-web for this epic; create a tech-debt ticket to merge back into the design system post-launch

internal.description: Accounts Platform team must expose the /api/v3/portfolio/summary endpoint with the aggregated total field — currently returns per-account data only
Client-ABC Identity Platform team must issue the portfolio:advisor_read OAuth scope for the advisor portal integration (FR-6)
Design System team AccountCard component extension for annuity account type and degraded-mode variant

external.description: Adobe Analytics Client-ABC implementation team must provision the client_abc_dashboard_loaded and client_abc_date_range_selected custom event slots in the analytics schema

definition_of_done: Dashboard is live for 100% of authenticated clients (LaunchDarkly flag fully rolled out, not just canary)
Call center volume for portfolio-balance-question call reason code is down ≥ 20% vs. 60-day pre-launch baseline (measured at 60 days post full rollout)
WCAG 2.1 AA audit passes with zero critical violations on all dashboard components (axe-core CI gate + manual screen reader test)
Lighthouse CI p95 LCP ≤ 2.5s and p95 TBT ≤ 300ms gates pass on production traffic sample
Compliance team has provided written sign-off on as-of timestamp and disclosure language (D-14)
All stories accepted by the product owner in JIRA and linked to this epic
Runbook for degraded-mode and rollback procedure documented in Confluence and reviewed by on-call team

story_outline.title: Portfolio summary aggregate total — layout shell and data fetch
story_outline.notes: Wire up TanStack Query to /api/v3/portfolio/summary; render aggregate total and as-of timestamp; no sparkline yet

story_outline.title: Day-over-day performance delta — dollar and percentage change display
story_outline.notes: Depends on story 1; add delta figures with icon+color treatment meeting WCAG AA

story_outline.title: Per-account breakdown rows below aggregate total
story_outline.notes: Extend AccountCard from design system; one row per account with type label, balance, day change

story_outline.title: 12-month sparkline with date-range selector
story_outline.notes: Recharts sparkline; wire to /api/v3/portfolio/history; date-range selector (1M 3M 6M 1Y YTD); text-table toggle for a11y

story_outline.title: Financial Advisor portal view — client dashboard with advisor banner
story_outline.notes: Requires portfolio:advisor_read scope from Identity Platform (dependency CABC-4803); advisor-view banner; 403 handling

story_outline.title: Degraded-mode fallback when aggregation service is unavailable
story_outline.notes: Show stale cached per-account balances with staleness warning; suppress sparkline; R-1 contingency

story_outline.title: LaunchDarkly feature flag integration and phased rollout wiring
story_outline.notes: Gate entire dashboard under client_abc_feature_client-portfolio-summary-dashboard flag; canary → 10% → 50% → 100% rollout plan
